titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 629*

p.seg: Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of the whole of his land sends greeting.

I have granted to the prior and monks of Coldingham, our well-beloved and sworn men, that no one may take poinds from them, or from their men on the demesne of the barony of Coldingham, for any debt or forfeit save for their own debt or forfeit.
Wherefore we forbid that anyone unjustly seek to trouble the said monks and their men against this our grant, on pain of our full forfeiture.
As witnesses: William, earl of Mar; Alexander, steward; David of Graham; Nicholas de Soules, sheriff of Roxburgh; Thomas son of Ranulf.
At Coldingham, 21 April, in the 34th year of the king's reign [1248].
